title: Devices, systems and methods for surface sampling
abstract: Devices, systems and methods are disclosed which relate to using wet foam elution for removal of particles from swabs and wipes. This allow users to capture particles from surfaces and recover them by elution into small sample volumes for subsequent detection for human clinical, veterinary, food safety, pharmaceutical, outbreak investigations, forensics, biodefense and bioterrorism response, environmental monitoring, and other applications where collection of samples from surfaces and humans or animals is required. More specifically, the swabs or wipes are used to collect samples in the standard ways that commercially available swabs and wipes are in use today; from, for instance, food preparation surfaces in food plants, from production equipment in pharmaceutical facilities, for collection of dry powders during bioterrorism event response, and for collection of clinical samples such as nasal, throat, nasopharyngeal, and wounds.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A portable system for collecting and extracting a sample from a surface, the system comprising: a swab having a head at one end of an extended shaft, the extended shaft having a lumen that extends substantially throughout the length of the shaft; a source of pressurized wet foam; wherein as the source of pressurized foam is introduced at an opposite end of the extended shaft with respect to the head, the wet foam travels through the lumen and elutes samples from the surface of the swab head. 2. The system in claim 1 , wherein the source of wet foam is a portable canister. 3. The system in claim 2, wherein the canister is activated by a wet foam dispensing lever. 4. The system in claim 1 , wherein the source of wet foam is a separate elution system. 5. The system in claim 1 , wherein the extended shaft includes apertures near the head to allow for uniform distribution of wet foam through the head and onto its exterior surface. 6. The system in claim 1 , further comprising a collection receptacle for collecting the wet foam with sample after elution from the swab head. 7. A portable system for collecting and extracting a sample from a surface, the system comprising: a swab having a head at one end of an extended shaft; a source of pressurized wet foam; a clam-shell cartridge which accommodates the swab head such that the swab head is substantially sealed within a chamber inside the closed cartridge; wherein as the source of pressurized foam is introduced into the cartridge, the wet foam elutes samples from the surface of the swab head. 8. The system in claim 7, wherein the cartridge has a hinge opposite of where the extended shaft of the swab extends therein. 9. The system in claim 8, wherein the cartridge has an accommodating aperture adapted to retain the extended shaft of the swab. 10. The system in claim 9, wherein the cartridge further comprises a collapsing reservoir distal to where the wet foam elutes samples from the surface of the swab head. 1 1 . The system in claim 7, wherein the source of wet foam is a portable canister. 12. The system in claim 7, wherein the source of wet foam is a separate elution system. 13. A portable system for collecting and extracting a sample from a surface, the system comprising: a wipe material held by a support structure; and a source of pressurized wet foam; wherein as the source of pressurized foam is introduced, the wet foam spreads substantially evenly through the wipe material to elute samples from the wipe material. 14. The system in claim 13, further comprising a collection receptacle for collecting the wet foam with sample after elution from the wipe material. 15. The system in claim 14, wherein the collection receptacle is flexible bag. 16. The system in claim 15, further comprising a sample aliquot reservoir positioned distal to the flexible bag wherein upon squeezing of the flexible bag, the sample dispenses into the aliquot reservoir. 17. The system of claim 16, further comprising a pinch valve positioned between the flexible bag and sample aliquot reservoir. 18. The system of claim 16, further comprising an adaptor positioned on a distal end of the sample aliquot reservoir to allow for direct coupling with a sample assay system. 19. The system in claim 15, wherein the source of wet foam is a portable canister. 20. The system in claim 15, wherein the source of wet foam is a separate elution system.
